Instruction Scheduling[指令调度]• Some factsuInstructions take clock cycles to execute (latency)uModern machines issue several operations per cycle (Out-of-Order Execution)uExecution time is order-dependent• Goal: reorder the operations to minimize execution timeuMinimize wasted cyclesuAvoid spilling registersuImprove locality
45
A= x * y;B = A+ 1;C = y;A= x * y;C = y;B = A+ 1;(Now C=y can execute while waiting for A=x*y)

Register Allocation[寄存器分配]•In TAC, there are an unlimited number of variablesu On a physical machine there are a small number of registers•Register allocation is the process of assigning variables to registers and managing data transfer in and out of registersu How to assign variables to finitely many registers?u What to do when the number of variables outweighs the number registers?u How to do so efficiently?•Using registers intelligently is a critical step in any compileru Accesses to memory are costly, even with caches u A good register allocator can generate code orders of magnitude better than a bad register allocator46

Register Allocation (cont.)•Goals of register allocationuKeep frequently accessed variables in registersuKeep variables in registers only as long as they are live •Local register allocation[局部]uAllocate registers basic block by basic blockuMakes decisions on a per-block basis (hence ‘local’) •Global register allocation[全局]u Makes global decisions about register allocation such thatp Var to reg mappings remain consistent across blocksp Structure of CFG is taken into account on decisions•Three well-known register allocation algorithms u Graph coloring allocator[图着⾊]u Linear scan allocator[线性扫描]u LP (Integer Linear Programming) allocator[整数线性规划] 47

Graph Coloring[图着⾊]• Register interference graph (RIG)[相交图]uEach node represents a variableuAn edge between two nodes V1 and V2 represents an interference in live ranges[活跃期/⽣存期]• Based on RIGuTwo variables can be allocated in the same register if there is no edge between them[若⽆边相连, 可使⽤同⼀寄存器]uOtherwise, they cannot be allocated in the same register • Problem of register allocation maps to graph coloringuOnce solved, k colors can be mapped back to k registersuIf the graph is k-colorable, it’s k-register-allocatable48

Register Spilling[寄存器溢出]• Determining whether a graph is k-colorable is NP-completeuTherefore, problem of k-register allocation is NP-completeuIn practice: use heuristic polynomial algorithm that gives sub-optimal allocations in most of the timeuChaitin’s graph coloring is a popular heuristic algorithm pe.g., most backends of GCC use Chaitin’s algorithm • What if k-register allocation does not exist?uSpill a variable to memory to reduce RIG and try againuSpilled variable stays in memory and is not allocated a register • Spilling is slowuPlaced into memory, loaded into register when needed, and written back to memory when no longer used49

Peephole Optimization[窥孔优化]•Optimization waysuUsual: produce good code through careful instruction selection and register allocationuAlternative: generate naive target code and then improve•A simple but effective technique for locally improving the target codeuDone by examining a sliding window of target instructions (called peephole) uReplace instruction sequences within the peephole by a shorter or faster sequenceuCan also be applied directly after IR generation to improve IR•Example transformationsuRedundant-instruction eliminationuFlow-of-control optimizationsuAlgebraic simplifications

Summary•Code can be optimized at different levels with various techniquesuPeephole, local, loop, globaluIR: local, global, CSE, constant folding and propagation, …uTarget: instruction, register, …•Interactions between the various optimization techniquesuSome transformations may expose possibilities for othersuOne optimization may hide or remove possibilities for others•Affect of compiler optimizations are intertwined and hard to separateuFinding optimal optimization combinations is in researchuCompilers package optimization that typically go together into levels (e.g., -O1, -O2)51
